breakout_products.title: Huddle01 VMs
breakout_products.content: Virtual Machines for Your Agents. Huddle01 Cloud allows you to spin up a Virtual Machine (VM) from your favourite AI assistant now! The MCP server lets you control your infrastructure setup on Huddle01 just by chatting. Works with Claude, Cursor, Antigravity, or anywhere you can set up MCPs. Your virtual machines come with Unlimited Ingress, Per-second billing, and Unthrottled NVMe Storage. Product categories: OpenClaw.
breakout_products.selection_reason: Innovative VM infrastructure controlled via MCP for AI agents with per-second billing.
breakout_products.summary: Huddle01 Cloud introduces a new Model Context Protocol server that enables users to provision and manage virtual machines entirely through conversational AI interfaces.
breakout_products.display_title: Deploy Virtual Machines Directly Through AI Assistants Using Huddle01 Cloud's New MCP Server
key_points: The system integrates with Claude, Cursor, and Antigravity to allow infrastructure control via natural language chat.
Users can deploy virtual machines specifically designed for AI agents using any platform that supports MCPs.
The service features per-second billing, unlimited ingress, and unthrottled NVMe storage for deployed virtual machines.

breakout_products.title: Rosentic
breakout_products.content: Catch when coding agents break each other before merge. Rosentic checks every PR against every other open PR before merge. When coding agents work in parallel, they break each other in ways no single-PR tool catches. Rosentic catches it. Deterministic analysis. Same scan, same result, every time. Most scans come back clean. That means you're clear to merge. Runs on your infrastructure. One YAML file, no signup, 60-second install. Product categories: Code Review Tools.
breakout_products.selection_reason: Solves a specific pain point: preventing parallel coding agents from breaking each other's PRs.
breakout_products.summary: This code review tool performs deterministic analysis by cross-checking every open pull request against each other to prevent parallel coding agents from causing conflicts before merging.
breakout_products.display_title: Automated Code Review Tool Prevents Parallel Coding Agents From Breaking Pull Requests Before Merging
key_points: Analyzes every open pull request against all others to identify conflicts missed by single-PR tools.
Provides deterministic analysis ensuring consistent and reliable scan results every time.
Deploys directly on user infrastructure via a single YAML file with a 60-second installation process and no signup required.

breakout_products.title: Radar
breakout_products.content: The missing open-source Kubernetes UI. Radar brings your Kubernetes workflows into one fast, open-source UI: real-time topology, resources, events, Helm, GitOps, live traffic flows, security & best-practice checks, image filesystem inspection, and MCP for AI agents. Run it locally as a single binary or self-host it in-cluster with RBAC + OIDC — no account, agents, or cloud required. Product categories: Observability tools.

hot_news.title: PORTool: Importance-Aware Policy Optimization with Rewarded Tree for Multi-Tool-Integrated Reasoning
hot_news.content: Multi-tool-integrated reasoning enables LLM-empowered tool-use agents to solve complex tasks by interleaving natural-language reasoning with calls to external tools. However, training such agents using outcome-only rewards suffers from credit-assignment ambiguity, obscuring wh...
hot_news.selection_reason: Apple research on optimizing multi-tool reasoning for AI agents using rewarded trees.
hot_news.summary: Apple researchers have developed PORTool, a new method designed to resolve credit-assignment ambiguity when training language models to use multiple external tools.
hot_news.display_title: Apple introduces PORTool to improve policy optimization for multi-tool reasoning in language models
key_points: The framework targets AI agents that interleave natural language reasoning with external tool calls.
It addresses the limitations and ambiguities of using outcome-only rewards during agent training.
The approach utilizes a rewarded tree structure to improve importance-aware policy optimization.

hot_news.title: Google just put a 2029 expiration date on Bitcoin's cryptography. And it is so dangerous they are...
hot_news.content: Google just put a 2029 expiration date on Bitcoin's cryptography. And it is so dangerous they are refusing to publish the code. We thought a quantum attack on crypto was decades away and it would take millions of qubits and massive computing power. But we were wrong.. Google Quantum AI just dropped a paper proving you don't need a futuristic supercomputer to break the elliptic curve cryptography that secures Bitcoin and Ethereum. You only need 1,200 logical qubits. That is a 20-fold reduction in the hardware previously thought necessary to crack the network. Here is the terrifying part: When you send Bitcoin, your public key is exposed in the "mempool" for about 10 minutes before the transaction is confirmed in a block. Google’s researchers compiled a quantum circuit that can theoretically derive your private key from that exposed public key in roughly 9 minutes. It takes the dreaded "mempool attack" and turns it into a mathematical reality. Now, take a deep breath. Existing crypto is not dead today. Google did not actually execute this hack. The physical hardware required to run this circuit (roughly 500,000 physical qubits) does not exist yet. Today's best quantum computers are only hovering around 1,000 physical qubits. But the math is officially solved and Google just proved exactly how to build the key.
hot_news.selection_reason: Google Quantum AI research proves theoretical path to breaking Bitcoin's cryptography.
hot_news.summary: A new paper from Google Quantum AI outlines a mathematical approach to cracking the elliptic curve cryptography used by Bitcoin and Ethereum with only 1,200 logical qubits.
hot_news.display_title: Google Quantum AI research demonstrates a theoretical method to break cryptocurrency encryption using fewer qubits
key_points: The theoretical attack reduces the required hardware for breaking crypto networks by a factor of twenty.
The proposed quantum circuit could theoretically derive a private key from an exposed public key in roughly nine minutes.
Executing the hack requires approximately 500,000 physical qubits, far exceeding the 1,000-qubit capacity of current quantum computers.

hot_news.title: Andrei Karpathy 最近红杉访谈解释他认为的软件3.0 软件 1.0：人写代码，代码定义规则，计算机执行规则。 软件 2.0： 用数据训练神经网络权重，人不再写规则，人喂数据，模型自...
hot_news.content: Andrei Karpathy 最近红杉访谈解释他认为的软件3.0 软件 1.0：人写代码，代码定义规则，计算机执行规则。 软件 2.0： 用数据训练神经网络权重，人不再写规则，人喂数据，模型自己学。 软件 3.0：编程核心杠杆变成了两个东西：提示词和上下文控制 https://t.co/WlTb0rH8sJ
hot_news.selection_reason: Karpathy defines 'Software 3.0' as prompt and context-driven engineering.
hot_news.summary: In a recent Sequoia interview, Andrej Karpathy outlined the evolution of programming into Software 3.0, where prompts and context control replace traditional coding.
hot_news.display_title: Andrej Karpathy Defines Software 3.0 as Prompting and Context Control in Recent Interview
key_points: Software 1.0 relied on humans writing explicit code and rules for computers to execute.
Software 2.0 shifted to training neural network weights using large datasets instead of manual rules.
Software 3.0 centers on using prompts and context management as the primary levers for programming.

llm.title: 🚨 A $2.5B startup just put Nvidia in a sandwich on the hardest document retrieval benchmark in AI...
llm.content: 🚨 A $2.5B startup just put Nvidia in a sandwich on the hardest document retrieval benchmark in AI. It's called webAI-ColVec1. And they open sourced it. Their 9B model sits at #1 on ViDoRe V3. Their 4B model sits at #3. Nvidia's best open-source embedding model is stuck at #2 between them. ViDoRe V3 is not a toy benchmark. 26,000+ document pages. 3,000+ human-verified queries. 10 enterprise domains. Financial filings, healthcare records, technical manuals, dense tables, messy layouts. The stuff that actually breaks production RAG systems. Here's what makes this different from everything else on the leaderboard: → Retrieves directly from rendered page images instead of extracted text → Skips OCR entirely. The model sees the page the same way you do → Tables, charts, scanned pages, dense layouts. All handled natively → Two model sizes: 4B for speed-sensitive edge deployments, 9B for max accuracy → Trained on ~2 million question-image pairs across scientific papers, financial filings, government reports, healthcare docs, and multilingual documents → Built on Qwen 3.5 vision-language backbones with LoRA adaptation → Trained on just 8 A100s with an effective batch size of 512 → Each query learns against 511 competing document pages per training step → Proprietary loss function that forces cleaner separation between correct and wrong pages → Multiple embedding sizes (128, 640, 2560) so you pick your own speed vs. quality tradeoff Here's the wildest part: Most enterprise teams are paying per-page and per-token fees just to get their documents into a format their RAG system can search. Reducto charges $0.015 per page for parsing. Cohere Embed v4 costs $0.12 per million tokens. Voyage AI's flagship model runs $0.18 per million tokens. And all of those still depend on OCR as the first step. One bad table extraction upstream and your entire retrieval pipeline breaks. webAI threw out that entire architecture. The model reads the page like a human. And it beats every paid and open-source alternative on the benchmark designed to test exactly that. This didn't come from a massive model or a giant infrastructure budget. 8 A100s. Deliberate training recipe. Retrieval-specific design. That's it. Cohere Embed v4: $0.12/million tokens. Voyage AI voyage-3-large: $0.18/million tokens. OpenAI text-embedding-3-large: $0.13/million tokens. This: Free. Open source. #1 on the leaderboard. @thewebAI 100% Open Source. (Link in the comments)
llm.selection_reason: webAI-ColVec1 open-sources vision-based embedding models that outperform Nvidia on RAG benchmarks.
llm.summary: A startup called webAI has released open-source embedding models that achieve top rankings on the ViDoRe V3 document retrieval benchmark by processing rendered page images directly instead of relying on text extraction.
llm.display_title: Open-source webAI models outperform Nvidia on complex document retrieval benchmarks without using OCR
key_points: The 9B and 4B models secured the first and third positions on the ViDoRe V3 benchmark, surpassing Nvidia's best open-source embedding model.
The architecture completely bypasses OCR, allowing the model to natively handle tables, charts, and dense layouts by reading page images.
The models were built on Qwen 3.5 vision-language backbones and trained efficiently using only eight A100 GPUs.

product_updates.title: A single 𝗖𝗟𝗔𝗨𝗗𝗘.𝗺𝗱 file just hit #1 on GitHub trending 🤯 It fixes LLMs' worst coding habits using...
product_updates.content: A single 𝗖𝗟𝗔𝗨𝗗𝗘.𝗺𝗱 file just hit #1 on GitHub trending 🤯 It fixes LLMs' worst coding habits using 4 principles from Karpathy: Karpathy called LLMs out for making wrong assumptions silently. They overcomplicate everything. They edit code they were never asked to change. No pushback. No clarifying questions. They just run. So those observations were encoded into 4 behavioral constraints: → Think before coding. If something’s ambiguous, ask. Don’t pick one interpretation and run. Surface tradeoffs, stop when confused. → Simplicity first. Write the minimum code that solves the problem. No speculative abstractions, no flexibility nobody asked for. → Surgical changes. Only touch what the task requires. Don’t improve neighboring code, don’t refactor what isn’t broken. → Goal-driven execution. Turn vague instructions into verifiable targets before writing a line. “Add validation” becomes “write tests for invalid inputs, then make them pass.” It works immediately. Drop the file in your project root and Claude Code follows it from the first task. One file. Zero dependencies. No setup. And best part, 100% open source.
product_updates.selection_reason: Trending CLAUDE.md file implements Karpathy's principles to improve LLM coding behavior.
product_updates.summary: A trending open-source CLAUDE.md file provides behavioral constraints for Claude Code to prevent overcomplication and unauthorized refactoring.
product_updates.display_title: Open-source configuration file applies Karpathy principles to fix common LLM coding mistakes
key_points: Enforces four core principles including simplicity, surgical changes, and goal-driven execution.
Requires the AI to ask clarifying questions instead of making silent assumptions.
Works immediately with zero dependencies by simply dropping the file into a project root.
